name: Sant Boi - Begues Trails Loop
description: This difficult 22.4-mile mountain biking route in the Garraf Massif offers challenging corriols and 2991 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sant Boi De Llobregat offers diverse terrain, situated between the Garraf Massif, the Ordal Mountains, and the Llobregat River delta. The region features a blend of urban, agricultural, forested, and riverine environments, providing varied landscapes for cycling. Sant Ramon Mountain, at 294 meters, is a prominent feature with Mediterranean forests of pine and holm oak. The area's moderate steepness and generally dry conditions contribute to well-maintained trails suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Sant Boi De Llobregat The most popular mountain bike trail is Sant Boi-corriols Begues, a 22.4 miles (36.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete, offering challenging sections through the local trail network. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Espacios Naturales delta del Llobregat - circular, an easy 13.8 miles (22.2 km) path. This route explores the protected natural areas of the Llobregat Delta, characterized by wetlands and lagoons. Local mountain bikers also love the Sant Ramon Hermitage – View of Rocky Cliff loop from Molí Nou | Ciutat Cooperativa, a 24.9 miles (40.1 km) trail leading through forested hills to the Sant Ramon Hermitage, often completed in about 4 hours 21 minutes. Mountain biking around Sant Boi De Llobregat is defined by the hilly terrain of the Baix Mountains, the Llobregat River delta, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more difficult singletracks. The routes in Sant Boi De Llobregat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sant Boi De Llobregat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Sant Boi de Llobregat, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: You can expect a varied landscape, from the hilly terrain of the Garraf Massif and Ordal Mountains to the flat, protected areas of the Llobregat River delta. The region features Mediterranean forests of pine and holm oak, agricultural zones, and riverine environments. The trails benefit from moderate steepness and generally dry conditions.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain biking trails in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: The trails cater to various skill levels. Approximately 5 routes are easy, 11 are moderate, and 9 are considered difficult. This means you can find everything from gentle riverside paths to more challenging singletracks through forested hills.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: The region's generally dry conditions make it suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures. The area is also known for observing migratory birds, especially in September and October, which can add to the experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952581169/espacios-naturales-delta-del-llobregat-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espacios Naturales delta del Llobregat - circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 22.2 km path exploring the protected natural areas of the Llobregat Delta. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat Footbridge – Llobregat River loop from Sant Boi&lt;/a&gt; is another easy option, offering a pleasant ride along the river.

Q: Can I find any waterfalls along the mountain bike routes?
A: While Sant Boi de Llobregat itself is not known for large waterfalls, there are some smaller cascades and gorges in the wider area. You might explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1423555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Mola&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1411041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of nearby guides focusing on waterfalls.

Q: Are there historical sites or castles accessible from the trails?
A: Yes, the region has historical points of interest. While mountain biking, you might encounter remnants of historical structures. For example, the area around Sant Boi de Llobregat includes highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/951471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellciuró Ruins&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6537362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eramprunyà Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of nearby guides focusing on castles.

Q: What natural parks or reserves are near the mountain biking trails?
A: Sant Boi de Llobregat is nestled between the Garraf Massif and the Ordal Mountains (Muntanyes del Baix). Portions of the protected Llobregat Delta Natural Areas, such as Les Basses de Can Dimoni, also fall within the municipality, offering unique biodiversity and wetlands.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Ramon Hermitage – View of Rocky Cliff loop from Molí Nou | Ciutat Cooperativa&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through forested hills to the Sant Ramon Hermitage. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952581169/espacios-naturales-delta-del-llobregat-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espacios Naturales delta del Llobregat - circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy route through the delta.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or natural features to look out for?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Ramon Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; at the peak of Muntanya de Sant Ramon offers panoramic views across the Llobregat Delta. The Vall de Santa Bàrbara is a beautiful forested valley with native trees. The Llobregat Delta itself provides excellent opportunities for birdwatching, especially for migratory species.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Sant Boi de Llobregat?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse terrain, which includes both challenging singletracks and easy riverside paths, as well as the scenic Mediterranean forests and the unique environment of the Llobregat Delta.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950583623/sant-boi-corriols-begues" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Boi-corriols Begues&lt;/a&gt; trail is a difficult 36.0 km route with significant elevation gain, offering challenging sections through the local trail network. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24218602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llobregat River – Playa de Viladecans loop from Sant Boi&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 51.6 km ride.
tips:
La Font de Golbes is a place located in the municipality of Sant Boi de Llobregat, on the way to the Hermitage of Sant Ramon, on the Montbaig mountain. In fact, in medieval times, Montbaig was called "Mountain of Golbes". Very close to the site is an archaeological site, which is also known by the same name.

It is a quiet square surrounded by nature, with a fountain that gives it its name, about 120 meters above sea level.
A beautiful route to the Ermita de Sant Ramon from various points in the Baix Llobregat.

The Chapel of the Virgen del Rosario or Ermita del Rosario is a hermitage located in Sant Climent de Llobregat that is part of the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia and was built in 1958.
It is an ideal place to go hiking or cycling and take refuge in the shadows of its trees.
Small church, in which we find a source of potable water, at the entrance to Begues

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Sant Boi-corriols Begues mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route is approximately 36 km (22.4 miles) long and typically takes around 3 hours and 53 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, riding speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or highlights.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The Sant Boi-corriols Begues route is rated as difficult. It features a significant elevation gain of over 900 meters and includes diverse terrain with 'corriols' (singletracks), gravel roads, dirt, and rocky paths. Riders should have good fitness and advanced riding skills, as some sections may be technically challenging.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a varied landscape on this route. Expect a mix of gravel roads, dirt paths, and rocky sections. The 'corriols' or singletracks can range from gentle stretches through the woods to more technical parts. The area is known for its spectacular mountains, offering a natural and engaging riding experience.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting spots. You can see the Golbes Fountain, the historic Hermitage of Sant Ramon, the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rosary, and the Rectoria de Begues Water Fountain. These offer great opportunities for a short break and to appreciate the local history and natural beauty.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride the Sant Boi-corriols Begues route?
faqs.answer: The Garraf Massif area offers year-round mountain biking opportunities. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and often stable weather conditions. Summers can be hot, so an early start is recommended, while winters are generally mild but can be cooler at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of the Camí Ral de Barcelona a Vilafranca, Camí de l'Ermita, Camí natural de l’Anell Verd - Golbes, Ruta del Sol Blau, GR 92-3, and the GR 92, among others. This allows for a diverse riding experience through established trail networks.

faqs.question: Is the Sant Boi-corriols Begues route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the Garraf Massif, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Given the route's difficult rating and varied terrain, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness and paw health.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to access the Garraf Massif?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for mountain biking on public trails within the Garraf Massif. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, fire prevention, and private property. It's advisable to check for any temporary restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Sant Boi. Specific parking information isn't provided, but you can typically find public parking in or near the town center of Sant Boi de Llobregat. For precise trailhead access, consult the Komoot tour map for the exact starting point and plan your parking accordingly.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, you'll be near the towns of Sant Boi and Begues, where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops for supplies. The Golbes Fountain and the Rectoria de Begues Water Fountain are listed highlights, suggesting potential water sources, but it's always best to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of this challenging ride.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating, significant distance, and elevation gain, this specific Sant Boi-corriols Begues route is generally not recommended for families with young children or beginner mountain bikers. The area around Begues and Sant Boi does offer easier trails, but this particular tour requires good fitness and advanced riding skills.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rosary
